label: Table 1
title: Comparison of demographic characteristics of OCD patients and healthy controls [N = (150 patients; 131 controls)].
source: Rai et al., 2022, Psychiatry Research Communications

columns.role: What is being compared (hover on other columns too)
Value for the 150 OCD participants—percent for sex rows, mean±SD for age and education
Same measure for the 131 healthy comparison participants
Test statistic: chi-square for counts like gender, t-test for continuous rows like age
p-value (probability such a gap could happen by chance); under 0.05 = authors treated it as statistically significant

footnote: © Mean ± standard deviation (for Age and Years of Education). Gender rows show n (%).
cellGuide: This table checks “did we accidentally compare apples to oranges?” Age, sex split, and education look similar when p-values are large—so later differences in behavioural screens are less likely to be simple demographic mismatch. Hover column headers for quick reminders of what each number means.
